ogTitle: Macdonald Park vs Macgillivray: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Macdonald Park and Macgillivray.
heading: Macdonald Park vs Macgillivray — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Macdonald Park, South Australia and Macgillivray, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Macgillivray has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Macdonald Park (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Macdonald Park or Macgillivray safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Macdonald Park has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Macgillivray scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Macdonald Park scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Macgillivray scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Macdonald Park or Macgillivray?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Macgillivray has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.28% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
